description_long: This house features a combination of white plaster and red brick exterior walls, with a prominent orange-tiled gable roof and several chimneys. The structure consists of two connected sections: a larger main volume and a smaller side extension, both with pitched roofs. Windows are rectangular and arranged in a mostly asymmetrical pattern, with some grouped in pairs and others spaced apart. The main entrance is located on the side extension, accessible via a small set of steps and a covered porch area. The house has visible rain gutters and downspouts along the roof edges, and the overall design is simple and functional, typical of mid-20th-century suburban or rural European homes. The building appears to be a single-family residence with modest decorative detailing.
description: A modest house with white plaster and red brick walls, orange-tiled gable roofs, and asymmetrical window placement. The structure includes a main section and a smaller side extension, with a simple entryway and visible chimneys.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main gable roof, three windows, and a green door on the white plaster facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the orange-tiled roof, a single window on the main section, and the red brick extension with two windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the red brick wall of the extension, a small window, and the back of the main house with visible rooflines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the entryway with steps, a covered porch, and additional windows on both the main and extension sections.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entryway with a single door, small set of steps, and a covered porch area without decorative columns.
